examples.title: Simple Parity Check Algorithm
examples.description: Implements even parity check for error detection
examples.code:
FUNCTION CalculateParityBit(data)
 count = 0
 FOR each bit in data
 IF bit == 1 THEN
 count = count + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 IF count % 2 == 0 THEN
 RETURN 0 // Even parity: no addition needed
 ELSE
 RETURN 1 // Odd: add 1 to make even
 END IF
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION VerifyParityWithErrorCheck(receivedData, parityBit)
 calculatedParity = CalculateParityBit(receivedData)
 IF calculatedParity == parityBit THEN
 RETURN "No Error Detected"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Error Detected"
 END IF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates even parity by counting 1s in data. If count is even, parity bit is 0; if odd, parity bit is 1. Receiver performs same calculation and compares. Mismatch indicates error. Can only detect odd number of bit flips.

examples.title: CRC Generation Using Polynomial Division
examples.description: Demonstrates CRC-8 generation using polynomial division
examples.code:
// CRC-8 Generator Polynomial: x^8 + x^2 + x + 1 (0x07)
CONSTANT POLYNOMIAL = 0x07

FUNCTION CalculateCRC8(dataBytes[])
 crc = 0x00
 FOR each byte in dataBytes
 crc = crc XOR byte
 FOR i = 0 TO 7
 IF (crc AND 0x80) != 0 THEN
 crc = (crc << 1) XOR POLYNOMIAL
 ELSE
 crc = crc << 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
 RETURN crc
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION TransmitFrame(dataBytes[])
 crc = CalculateCRC8(dataBytes)
 frame = dataBytes + [crc] // Append CRC
 RETURN frame
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION VerifyFrame(receivedFrame[])
 data = receivedFrame[0 to length-2]
 receivedCRC = receivedFrame[length-1]
 calculatedCRC = CalculateCRC8(data)
 IF calculatedCRC == receivedCRC THEN
 RETURN "Frame OK - No Error"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Error Detected - Request Retransmission"
 END IF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: CRC uses polynomial division to generate check bits. The generator polynomial is agreed upon by sender and receiver. CRC is appended to data. Receiver divides received frame by same polynomial - if remainder is zero, no error detected. Detects all single-bit errors and burst errors up to polynomial degree.

examples.title: Hamming Code Encoding and Error Correction
examples.description: Implements Hamming code for single-bit error detection and correction
examples.code:
// Hamming Code (7,4) - 4 data bits, 3 parity bits
FUNCTION EncodeHamming(dataBits[4])
 // Positions: 1=P1, 2=P2, 3=D1, 4=P4, 5=D2, 6=D3, 7=D4
 encoded[7]
 
 // Extract data bits
 encoded[3] = dataBits[0] // D1 at position 3
 encoded[5] = dataBits[1] // D2 at position 5
 encoded[6] = dataBits[2] // D3 at position 6
 encoded[7] = dataBits[3] // D4 at position 7
 
 // Calculate parity bits
 encoded[1] = encoded[3] XOR encoded[5] XOR encoded[7] // P1 covers D1,D2,D4
 encoded[2] = encoded[3] XOR encoded[6] XOR encoded[7] // P2 covers D1,D3,D4
 encoded[4] = encoded[5] XOR encoded[6] XOR encoded[7] // P4 covers D2,D3,D4
 
 RETURN encoded
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION DetectAndCorrect(encoded[7])
 errorPosition = 0
 
 // Check each parity bit
 parity1 = encoded[1] XOR encoded[3] XOR encoded[5] XOR encoded[7]
 IF parity1 == 1 THEN errorPosition = errorPosition + 1
 
 parity2 = encoded[2] XOR encoded[3] XOR encoded[6] XOR encoded[7]
 IF parity2 == 1 THEN errorPosition = errorPosition + 2
 
 parity4 = encoded[4] XOR encoded[5] XOR encoded[6] XOR encoded[7]
 IF parity4 == 1 THEN errorPosition = errorPosition + 4
 
 IF errorPosition > 0 THEN
 encoded[errorPosition] = NOT encoded[errorPosition] // Flip error bit
 RETURN "Error at position " + errorPosition + ", Corrected"
 ELSE
 RETURN "No Error Detected"
 END IF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Hamming code encodes 4 data bits using 3 parity bits at positions 1,2,4 (powers of 2). Each parity bit covers specific data positions. For error detection, recalculate parity bits and form binary number indicating error position. If errorPosition is 3, bit 3 is corrupted. Flip the bit to correct. Can detect and correct single-bit errors.
